methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $145M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Raphe mPhibr is a Series B-stage defense and security technology company founded by Vivek and Vikash Mishra that has raised $145M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage. Primary private comparables include Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, all Series B–stage defense tech firms.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($145M) as a proxy for post-money value and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yields $276M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables establishes a median valuation of $1.2B, with a documented range of $1.0B to $1.5B. The peer set includes valuations at $1.5B (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security), $1.4B (Harmattan AI), and $1.0B (Castelion, Defense Unicorns).

The triangulated valuation range is $234M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $738M. The wide spread reflects material divergence between stage-progression and peer-median methods, indicating sensitivity to methodology selection and peer comparability assumptions. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer cohort relative to Raphe's specific technology and market position, and whether total raised adequately proxies for prior post-money valuations.
